trans-unit.note: Backend module title as it appears in the TYPO3 main navigation under "Tools". Keep short; this is a navigation label. "Image Optimize" is the extension name (product name), it may stay untranslated if the target language commonly uses English technical terms.
Page heading of the main backend module view. "Processed images" refers to the on-the-fly generated image variants stored in /processed. "Maintenance" = administration/housekeeping.

trans-unit.source: Manage and clear on-demand processed images generated by EXT:nr_image_optimize.
trans-unit.note: Sub-heading under the module title. "EXT:nr_image_optimize" is the extension identifier, do NOT translate. "On-demand" = generated when first requested.
Card header/button label that triggers deletion of all processed image variants. "Clear" here means "delete/empty", not "make transparent".

trans-unit.source: Are you sure you want to delete all processed images? This action cannot be undone.
trans-unit.note: JavaScript confirmation dialog text shown before the destructive "Clear all processed images" action. Avoid single quotes/apostrophes that would break a plain string; use typographic quotation marks if possible.
Statistics label: number of sub-folders inside the /processed directory.
Statistics label for the total disk space occupied by all processed images (bytes).
Statistics label: the processed file with the earliest modification date.
Statistics label: the processed file with the most recent modification date.
Card header for the "5 biggest files" list. The number 5 is fixed in code; keep "(Top 5)" or adapt phrasing (e.g. "Top 5 largest files").
Table column header for file extension (jpg, png, webp, ...). Short label - keep one word.
Table column header for the number of files of a given type. Short label - keep one word.

trans-unit.source: Processed images have been cleared successfully
trans-unit.note: TYPO3 flash message (success) shown after the /processed directory was emptied successfully. No trailing period (TYPO3 style).

trans-unit.source: An error occurred while clearing processed images
trans-unit.note: TYPO3 flash message (error) shown when deletion of the /processed directory failed. No trailing period (TYPO3 style).
Button label to open the "system requirements" sub-view, and also the heading of that view.

trans-unit.source: Technical prerequisites and current system status
trans-unit.note: Sub-heading on the system requirements page, describing the list of checks (PHP, ImageMagick, GD, Composer, CLI tools).
Back button on the system requirements view that returns to the main maintenance view. "Maintenance" refers to the module, see module.maintenance.title.
Dashboard card label: total number of files in the /processed directory (tree-wide, including sub-directories).
Dashboard card label: total disk space used by the /processed directory. Displayed alongside a human-readable size (e.g. "24.53 MB").
Card header introducing storage metadata (path, oldest/newest file).
Label next to a read-only input field showing the absolute filesystem path of /processed.
Empty-state message shown when the /processed directory exists but contains no files yet.
Card header for a table that groups files by their extension and shows counts/total sizes per type.
Table column header for file size (individual or group total, human-readable). Short label.
Empty-state row inside tables (file types, largest files) when there is nothing to display.
Table column header for the relative path of a processed file (relative to /processed).
Lead-in (bold prefix) of a warning callout that explains the consequences of clearing. Keep the trailing colon.

trans-unit.source: After clearing, all processed images will be regenerated on-demand when first accessed. This may temporarily increase loading times on the frontend.
trans-unit.note: Body of the warning callout shown next to flash.clear.notice.title. Explains that on-demand regeneration will cost CPU on first hit.
Label of the destructive submit button that triggers deletion of the whole /processed directory.
Table column header on the system requirements page: name of the checked component (PHP version, extension, library ...). Short label.
Table column header on the system requirements page: badge column (Available / Limited / Missing). Short label.
Table column header: currently detected value (e.g. installed version string). Short label.
Table column header: minimum version / required state. Short label.
Table column header for a row-level info icon that reveals additional technical details via a tooltip. Short label.
Green status badge meaning: component is installed and meets the requirement.
Yellow status badge meaning: component is missing but optional, or only partially supports the required features.
Red status badge meaning: a required component is not installed or not working.
Heading of the legend that explains the three status badges. Keep the trailing colon.

trans-unit.source: Available: Component is installed and meets requirements
trans-unit.note: Legend line for the green badge. Format: "Label: explanation". Keep the colon after the badge label.

trans-unit.source: Limited: Component is optional or has limited functionality
trans-unit.note: Legend line for the yellow badge. Format: "Label: explanation".

trans-unit.source: Missing: Required component is not available
trans-unit.note: Legend line for the red badge. Format: "Label: explanation".
HTTP 503 response body when the middleware cannot acquire the processing lock. Shown to frontend visitors; keep short and user-friendly.
Row label on the system requirements page. "PHP" is a product name, do NOT translate.

Row label; the %s placeholder is replaced with the extension identifier (e.g. "imagick", "gd", "mbstring", "exif"). Keep the %s placeholder and the colon. "PHP" and "Extension" refer to PHP's PECL/C extensions.
Short "Current" value for a PHP extension that is loaded (active) in the running PHP process.
Short "Current" value for a PHP extension that is not active in the running PHP process.
Card header grouping all PHP-related rows (version + extensions).
Row label showing the version of the PECL "imagick" PHP extension. "Imagick" is a product name.
Row label showing the version of the underlying ImageMagick library (different from the PHP Imagick extension above). "ImageMagick" is a product name.
Row label indicating whether WebP format encoding/decoding is available. "WebP" is a product name, do NOT translate.
Row label indicating whether AVIF format encoding/decoding is available. "AVIF" is a product name, do NOT translate.
Affirmative "Current" value. Used as a short cell content indicating feature/format is supported.
Negative "Current" value. Used as a short cell content indicating feature/format is not supported.
Short "Required" value indicating that the component is not mandatory. Rendered inside a small muted badge.
Row label; the "Current" column then lists comma-separated image format identifiers (AVIF, WEBP, JPEG, ...).
Lowercase "Current" value used when a feature/library could not be queried. Rendered inside a code-style pill.
Short "Required" value meaning: optional, but strongly suggested for best results.
Card header grouping all rows about the ImageMagick/Imagick driver. "ImageMagick" is a product name. "Primary Driver" = this extension's preferred image backend.
Row label showing the version string of the PHP GD library. "GD" is a product name.
Card header grouping all rows about the GD image driver. "GD" is a product name. "Fallback Driver" = used when ImageMagick is not available.
Short "Required" value meaning: only relevant if the primary driver is missing.
Short "Current" value for a Composer dependency or CLI tool that is not present.
Card header grouping Composer package checks. "Composer" is a product name, do NOT translate.
Row label showing the running TYPO3 core version. "TYPO3" is a product name, do NOT translate.
Card header grouping TYPO3 core version checks.
Row label; checks whether PHP's shell command functions are enabled (needed to detect external CLI tools). Keep "PHP" and the function name shown in parentheses as-is.
Short "Current" value when a feature is enabled/turned on.
Short "Current" value when a feature is disabled/turned off.
Card header grouping optional external CLI binary checks (magick, convert, identify, gm). "CLI" = command line interface.
Row label for the ImageMagick 7+ "magick" command line binary. "magick" is the binary name, do NOT translate. "CLI:" indicates a command-line tool.
Row label for the legacy ImageMagick "convert" command line binary. "convert" is the binary name, do NOT translate.
Row label for the ImageMagick "identify" command line binary (reports image metadata). "identify" is the binary name, do NOT translate.
Row label for the GraphicsMagick "gm" binary (alternative to ImageMagick). "gm" is the binary name and "GraphicsMagick" is a product name, do NOT translate.
Short "Current" value for a CLI binary that was detected on the system PATH.
Short "Current" value for a CLI binary that could not be located on the system PATH.
Lowercase "Current" value used when a version could not be determined. Rendered inside a code-style pill.
